ops.text:
AMENDMENT (ratified, mid-run — the lane flagged a structural surprise and its fix is BETTER than the pre-reg; approved). Two pre-reg errors the lane caught: (1) the frozen steer-type frame is EVAL-ONLY — all 250 rows split="test" (built from the 452 traces-TEST rows), like the n=628 watcher frame; there is no train split IN the frame (my "train/test split via the frame's split" was wrong). (2) The frame's `category` labels ARE the opus judge's own output, so an "incumbent LLM judge" arm scored against them = macro-F1 1.0 by construction (vacuous, circular). The real judge-vs-HUMAN number (E30.3's 0.59 κ / 65.4% raw) lives on the SEPARATE auditor-gold-v1 set, which barely overlaps the frame (17/250 row-level).

RATIFIED two-experiment design (both $0-LLM, local): EXP A (primary/task-specified) — train encoder/TF-IDF/majority on traces/TRAIN judge labels (sessions of ALL eval sets excluded → ~3348 rows), eval on the frozen 250 (judge gold); the judge incumbent is flagged VACUOUS. Answers "can a cheap encoder DISTILL the opus judge's 11-way category?" EXP B (the meaningful incumbent test) — eval on auditor-gold-v1 TEST (191 rows resolvable to identical steer_type_text), HUMAN auditor_category gold, real incumbent = stored triage_category (recovers ~65% acc). Answers "does a cheap encoder MATCH the expensive LLM at agreeing with the human auditor?" (encoder ceiling ≈ the judge's human-agreement, since it's judge-distilled — the question is retention at ~0 cost, not exceeding). REQUIREMENTS I add: session-disjointness across ALL eval sets (frozen 250 AND auditor-gold 191) verified; BOTH A and B reported; the deviations (experiment-local multiclass head since encoder.py is binary; MPS not CPU; left-truncation as steer is at the text tail) noted in the report and checked by VERIFY. This is a stronger E43 than the pre-reg — the frame-is-a-distillation-target insight is itself a finding.
